EN-ES Printable Resource

My Clothes Book: Learning English & Spanish

A vocabulary pack designed for K-2 ELL newcomers to learn basic clothing words in English and Spanish, featuring simple visuals and engaging activities.

TopicClothes
Age BandK-2
Pack Typevocabulary pack
LanguageEN-ES

✨ What's Inside

  • 12 vocabulary items
  • 3 worksheets
  • Mini-book (8 pages)
  • Teacher notes & answer key

📖 How to Use

Introduce vocabulary using flashcards and realia. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use worksheets for practice and reinforcement. Encourage total physical response (TPR) by having students point to their own clothes or miming putting on clothes.

Print double-sided, fold in half, and staple to create a mini-book.

Worksheet 1

matching
Name: ____________________

Match the English word to the correct picture. Draw a line from the word to the picture.

Une la palabra en inglés con la imagen correcta. Dibuja una línea de la palabra a la imagen.

English

2pants
3dress
4shoes
5hat
6socks

Bzapatos

Esombrero

Worksheet 3

coloring

Color the clothes. Say the name of each item as you color.

LanternELLTeacher Notes📎 Sample

Teacher Notes

🎯 Objective

Students will be able to identify and name 12 common clothing items in English and Spanish, and use them in simple sentences.

💡 Suggested Use

Introduce vocabulary using flashcards and realia. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use worksheets for practice and reinforcement. Encourage total physical response (TPR) by having students point to their own clothes or miming putting on clothes.

📦 Materials Needed

  • Flashcards of clothing items
  • Real clothing items (if possible)
  • Crayons or colored pencils
  • Scissors (for cutting out mini-book)
  • Stapler (for mini-book)

🔀 Differentiation Tips

  • For struggling learners: Focus on 3-5 core vocabulary words. Provide sentence stems (e.g., 'This is a ______.') for speaking practice. Pair with a stronger peer.
  • For advanced learners: Encourage describing clothes with colors and sizes (e.g., 'This is a red shirt.'). Ask 'What do you wear when it's cold/hot?' Have them create their own simple sentences or draw additional clothing items.

Answer Key

ws-1

1.a red t-shirt
2.blue denim pants
3.a pink dress with short sleeves
4.a pair of brown shoes
5.a yellow baseball cap
6.a pair of striped socks
